https://khalid-salad.github.io/blog/dist.body:
Curse Words
        I will censor all the cursewords for now. I will try and find a toggle.

    

One of the worst things about reading stuff from the internet is this expectation that, because you are probably reading this
in a web browser, it is cool to add stupid distractions like footnotes1 and urls to unrelated bull****.

    Did you know that the modern web is a great way to train your neck muscles?


Notice, for example, that simply linking to something gives an unintended emphasis. At best,
this is distracting, but in many cases it changes the tone of the text.

    You might have thought “the next paragraph will continue the topic being discussed”, but you would be wrong. The current
best practice is to distract your readers so much that they can’t follow your writing, then have them eviscerate you in
your evaluations and on ratemyprofessors.


Similarly, the mere presence of a footnote can be distracting. For example, try and read the rest of this paragraph, but
disregard the upcoming footnote. I am not a big fan of Lorem Ipsum, because it just makes me feel insecure about the
fact that the only Latin I know is “post hoc ergo propter hoc”2 which roughly translates to “I studied
Spanish in high school.”3
With that in mind, I approach my writing with:

A considerate approach to distractions. If the material you are serving is meant to be read, then it should, disregarding markup, be mostly plaintext.
A commitment to accessibility. The tabi theme used for this site is accessible. Currently, math and diagrams are rendered clientside using KaTeX and Mermaid. I hope to eventually have everything rendered serverside (with something like MathJax, which has a reputation for accessibility). The ideal would be to have the website fully functional with no JavaScript.
Respect for the reader. External links will be kept to a minimum and clearly indicated (for example, here is an
external link to Daniel Podgorski’s excellent Unchosen Undead: A Thorough Existentialist Philosophical Analysis of FromSoftware’s Original Dark Souls).




see how this **** interrupts your flow while reading? ↩


ok i also know the initials QED ↩


this one, at the end of the paragraph, is noticeably less distracting. There is an art to
this, but i am not a good writer ↩
https://khalid-salad.github.io/blog/dist.description: My Commitment to Not Writing like ****
https://khalid-salad.github.io/blog/dist.title: On Writing Littered with ****ing Distractions

Introduction

an algorithm for determing the strongly connected components of a directed graph.

https://khalid-salad.github.io/projects/.body:
LeetCode is terrible, from the way it encourages this moronic culture of whiteboarding to the outright
awful solutions they post. See my page on describing algorithms.
In the interest of putting a dent in this, as well as providing a useful public good, I will write up solutions to
LeetCode exercises, with technical explanations on correctness and runtime where appropriate. The solutions are in
Python because I can just indent my pseudocode and it usually passes the test cases.
https://khalid-salad.github.io/projects/.description: A spiteful treatment of LeetCode exercises.
https://khalid-salad.github.io/projects/.title: LeetCode Is Bull****
